mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, corporate earnings growth expectations deteriorate sharply due to weak consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 8.0, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE is between 7.5 and 8.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand recovery, which forces equity markets to reprice earnings upward over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50 percent of instances, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates capacity utilization improvements that precede short-term equity rallies as investors price in operational leverage.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative 5-day returns in 60 percent of recovery-phase observations, or predictive accuracy drops below random chance when capacity utilization exceeds 80 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Lower inflation readings reduce real interest rate pressures and improve corporate profit margins, triggering immediate positive market repricing over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with CPI declines, or the signal fails to predict direction in 70 percent of low-inflation months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent signals insufficient aggregate demand to sustain corporate revenue expansion, causing equity markets to decline over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in over 60 percent of quarters where GDP growth is below 1.5 percent, or the threshold effect vanishes when adjusted for seasonal factors.

mechanism_narrative: High unemployment combined with persistently low policy rates indicates a liquidity trap where monetary stimulus fails to stimulate hiring, leading to prolonged equity underperformance.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive 60-day returns in 55 percent of cases, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding 95 percent of trend during a late-cycle expansion reflects peak operational efficiency that supports corporate earnings and drives positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in 65 percent of late-cycle months when INDPRO surpasses 95, or the signal loses predictive power when capacity utilization falls below 75 percent.
